title: Magnet Shape Matching
instructions_for_student: Drag each name label to the correct magnet shape outline. After matching all three, read the fun fact about each shape.

additionalProps.title: Magnet Shapes
additionalProps.subtitle: Chapter 4 – Exploring Magnets
additionalProps.instructionText: Drag each name label to the correct magnet shape outline. After matching all three, read the fun fact about each shape.

shapes.label: Bar Magnet
shapes.fact: Bar magnets are the most common shape used in school laboratories.
shapes.hint: Think of a straight rectangular block — like a ruler-shaped magnet.

shapes.label: U-shaped Magnet
shapes.fact: U-shaped (horseshoe) magnets concentrate both poles close together for a stronger pull.
shapes.hint: Think of the letter U — it curves at the bottom with two arms pointing up.

shapes.label: Ring Magnet
shapes.fact: Ring magnets have a hole in the centre and are used in speakers and electric motors.
shapes.hint: Think of a doughnut or a washer — a circle with a hole through the middle.

schema.description: JSON schema for the Magnet Matching Tool — drag-and-label activity

title.description: Main heading shown in the header banner

subtitle.description: Chapter or subject tag shown above the title

instructionText.description: Student-facing instructions shown inside the header

shapes.description: Array of shape definitions to match. Each item has an id, label, fact, and hint.

id.description: Shape identifier — controls which SVG outline is drawn

label.description: Name of the magnet shape shown on the draggable label

fact.description: One-line fun fact revealed after a correct match

hint.description: Short hint shown when a label is dropped on the wrong shape

quick_reference.purpose: Interactive drag-and-label activity for matching magnet shape SVG outlines to their names
key_features: Three inline-SVG magnet shape outlines: bar, U-shaped, ring
Draggable name labels with grab-cursor and hover animations
Correct matches lock in amber/green with a one-line fun fact
Wrong drops bounce back the shape zone and the label with a hint
Summary screen on completion showing all shapes with labels and facts
Two-column responsive layout — stacks on mobile
Zero external icon or UI library dependencies — all SVGs inline

shape_ids.bar: Rectangular bar magnet — horizontal rectangle with N/S labels at each end
shape_ids.u: U-shaped / horseshoe magnet — two vertical arms joined by a curve at the bottom
shape_ids.ring: Ring magnet — concentric circles (outer ring with centre hole), N/S at top/bottom

shapes.label: Bar Magnet
shapes.fact: Bar magnets are the most common shape used in school laboratories.
shapes.hint: Think of a straight rectangular block.

error_handling.unknown_shape_id: The ShapeCard component falls through to null — only 'bar', 'u', 'ring' render SVGs
error_handling.empty_shapes_array: Falls back to DEFAULT_SHAPES defined inside the component
